TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Dwarf Hold Faction.

Translations.Comment: Formal term for the faction strongholds; 'fortalezas' conveys strength and military purpose.
Standard infantry unit; straightforward military terminology.
Experienced soldiers; 'veteranos' implies seasoned combat experience.
Worker-soldiers from the mines; maintains industrial heritage of dwarves.
Ranged infantry with crossbows; 'ballesteros' is the traditional Spanish term.
Firearm-armed soldiers; 'mosqueteros' evokes fantasy gunpowder troops.
Scout/ranger units; 'exploradores' emphasizes their reconnaissance role.
Artillery piece; standard military terminology, singular form for equipment.
Siege weapon; generic term fitting fantasy wargame aesthetic.
Siege artillery; 'lanzapiedras' is a single compound word for impact.
Elite melee units; kept from English as 'berserker' is recognized in Spanish fantasy contexts.
Elite unit from deep dwarf holds; poetic yet martial tone.
Magical artillery; 'infernal' conveys both fire and otherworldly power.
Fantasy war machine; 'máquina voladora' maintains the steam/dwarf-tech aesthetic.
Leader/commander type; 'capataz' implies authority and craftsmanship.
Officer rank; 'capitán explorador' maintains military hierarchy.
Specialist unit; reflects dwarven expertise in construction and mechanics.
